critic_raw:
This design compares ResNet-50 against logistic regression on handcrafted features for cancer cell detection in microscopy images.

The first critical flaw is the retraining on combined train+validation sets after model selection. Section 6 states: 'both models are re-trained on the combined training + validation sets (9,500 images) to maximize use of available data.' When the final model is retrained on train+validation combined, the hyperparameters selected on the validation set are no longer appropriate — they were selected for a model trained on 7,000 images, not 9,500 images. For the CNN specifically, optimal learning rate, number of epochs, and early stopping patience can change substantially with 35% more training data. The final test evaluation is therefore being performed on a model that was not directly validated with those hyperparameters on that data size, making the test result an approximation at best and a biased estimate at worst.

Second, the normalization statistics are computed on the entire dataset before splitting. Section 3 states pixel values are normalized using statistics 'computed on the entire dataset before splitting,' and Section 3 also states handcrafted features are 'standardized using statistics fit on the entire dataset before splitting.' This is the same leakage pattern identified in multiple other cases: test-set distributional properties contaminate the normalization applied during training.

Third, the consensus label panel re-labels only 500 randomly selected images, not the test set specifically. The test set performance is primarily evaluated against single-pathologist labels (the remaining 9,500 images retain original labels). Sensitivity to label noise is only measured indirectly. If the CNN has learned to mimic the idiosyncratic labeling patterns of the original pathologist rather than true cancer presence, test-set performance on single-pathologist labels will be inflated — and the 3-pathologist consensus analysis (which would reveal this) is only performed on a random subset, not the full test set.

Fourth, the primary metric is sensitivity, and the baseline logistic regression hyperparameter C is tuned by 5-fold cross-validation using sensitivity as the selection criterion — but on the training set only. The CNN uses early stopping based on validation sensitivity. These are different optimization landscapes for the same metric, and the logistic regression search over 6 values of C with sensitivity-maximizing cross-validation is not equivalent in scope to full neural network hyperparameter search. Sensitivity on a small imbalanced test set of 1,500 images can vary substantially with threshold choice, and the design does not state whether both models are evaluated at a common threshold or at their individually optimal thresholds.
all_issues_raised: Both models are retrained on the combined training + validation set after hyperparameter selection, but the selected hyperparameters (learning rate, early stopping patience, epochs) were calibrated for a model trained on 7,000 images; retraining on 9,500 images with validation-selected hyperparameters invalidates the model selection and introduces an uncontrolled variable into the final test evaluation.

Pixel-level normalization and handcrafted feature standardization statistics are computed from the entire dataset before splitting, meaning test-set distributional properties contaminate preprocessing applied during training.

The 3-pathologist consensus re-labeling covers only a random 500-image subset, not the full test set; if the CNN learns to mimic single-pathologist idiosyncrasies, test performance against single-pathologist labels will be inflated and the re-labeling analysis will not detect it.

Sensitivity is the primary metric but the two models optimize it through different procedures (LR via cross-validation on training set; CNN via early stopping on validation set), and no common decision threshold is specified for the final test evaluation.
all_issues_adjudicated: Both models are retrained on the combined training + validation set after hyperparameter selection, but the selected hyperparameters (learning rate, early stopping patience, epochs) were calibrated for a model trained on 7,000 images; retraining on 9,500 images with validation-selected hyperparameters invalidates the model selection and introduces an uncontrolled variable into the final test evaluation.

Pixel-level normalization and handcrafted feature standardization statistics are computed from the entire dataset before splitting, meaning test-set distributional properties contaminate preprocessing applied during training.

The 3-pathologist consensus re-labeling covers only a random 500-image subset, not the full test set; if the CNN learns to mimic single-pathologist idiosyncrasies, test performance against single-pathologist labels will be inflated and the re-labeling analysis will not detect it.

Sensitivity is the primary metric but the two models optimize it through different procedures (LR via cross-validation on training set; CNN via early stopping on validation set), and no common decision threshold is specified for the final test evaluation.
